todos.title: Create private GitHub repo for Atlas
todos.notes: Make sure .gitignore excludes: .env, data/health/, data/budget/, config/integrations.yaml, data/prices/history/

todos.title: Set up GitHub Actions: weekly npm audit + lint on PR
todos.notes: Two workflows: (1) scheduled weekly audit of price-tracker and dashboard packages, (2) lint check on pull requests

todos.title: Plan home network zones (draw diagram)
todos.notes: Zones needed: Main LAN, IoT, Guest, Management (Pi). Use Pi-hole for DNS on all zones. Document VLAN IDs and IP ranges.

todos.title: Order cabling materials and patch panel
todos.notes: Cat6A for future-proofing. Figure out cable runs first. Check if existing wall boxes can be reused.

todos.title: Set up Gmail + Calendar MCP OAuth credentials
todos.notes: Go to Google Cloud Console → create project → enable Gmail API + Google Calendar API → create OAuth 2.0 credentials (Desktop app) → add GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET to .env

todos.title: Authenticate Google Calendar MCP in Claude.ai
todos.notes: Open claude.ai → type /mcp → select 'claude.ai Google Calendar' → complete OAuth flow. Then tell Atlas 'authenticated' — 61 events (Bern holidays + shooting schedule + training plan) will be added in one shot.

todos.title: Enhance Atlas monitoring: container metrics, heatmap, per-route health probes
todos.notes: Completed 2026-05-06. Added Blackbox Exporter (per-route UP/DOWN probes for 9 routes), cAdvisor (container CPU/RAM metrics), updated prometheus.yml with both scrape jobs, created atlas-monitoring Grafana dashboard (6 panels: page health, response time, container CPU, container RAM, OOM events, scrape health). All 11 Prometheus targets verified UP.

todos.title: Audit Atlas repo organisation + engage implementation specialist
todos.notes: Spawn software architect + implementation specialist to: (1) audit directory layout, naming, separation of concerns; (2) flag anything that doesn't scale or violates conventions; (3) propose a reorganisation plan if needed. Output: written reorganisation proposal in output/.

todos.title: Establish quarterly Atlas tech-stack + organisation health-check routine
todos.notes: Define a repeatable checklist (or cron) that: checks for outdated npm packages, reviews directory layout against current usage, flags dead code/agents/tools, and outputs a one-page health report. Run quarterly — next due 2026-08-01.

todos.title: Build exercise library page
todos.notes: Dedicated page to browse all 17 exercises. UX spec: two-column split panel (list left, existing detail panel right). Search bar + chip filters (muscle group + category). Dense list rows showing name, primary muscle chip, category tag. Add 'Used in: Session A/B' line to detail panel. Route: /exercises. Consider integrating Everkinetic SVGs (CC BY-SA) for form guide where available, YouTube embeds for gaps.

todos.title: Trading bot — research trading strategies beyond trailing stop
todos.notes: Research and catalogue strategies suitable for an algo bot. Beyond trailing stop: (1) Mean reversion — RSI oversold/overbought, Bollinger Band squeeze entries. (2) Momentum / trend following — EMA crossover (9/21), MACD signal cross, breakout from consolidation range. (3) News-driven — buy/sell trigger on sentiment score from headline feed within 60s of release. (4) Whale follow — enter same direction as large dark pool print above threshold within 5 min. (5) Politician lag — enter same direction as Congress trade disclosure, holding for 30-day window (historically high alpha). (6) Opening range breakout — 9:30–9:45 range established, trade breakout at 9:45 with stop at range midpoint. (7) Options flow signal — unusual call/put ratio spike as directional signal. (8) Earnings gap fade or follow — decide pre-earnings whether to fade the gap or ride it based on IV crush estimate. For each: note edge, typical holding period, required data feeds, risk. Output a strategy comparison doc.

todos.title: Trading bot — design & stack decision
todos.notes: Design a personal trading bot. Key components to decide on: (1) Execution: Alpaca Markets API (alpaca.markets) — commission-free US stocks + crypto, REST + WebSocket, paper trading available for free, no minimum. Best starting point for algo trading. (2) Market data: Alpaca Data API (free tier: 15-min delayed; paid: real-time). Polygon.io as alternative for options + tick data. (3) News feed: NewsAPI, Benzinga API, Alpha Vantage news endpoint, or Unusual Whales news. (4) Whale / unusual activity: Unusual Whales API (options flow, dark pool prints, whale alerts). Whalemap for crypto. (5) Politician trades: Quiver Quantitative API (Congress trading disclosures, real-time Capitol Trades feed). (6) Regulatory / law changes: Federal Register API (federalregister.gov/api/v1) for rule changes; SEC EDGAR full-text search for new filings. Output: architecture decision record (ADR) in output/.

todos.title: Trading bot — set up Alpaca paper trading account
todos.notes: Go to alpaca.markets → sign up for free paper trading account → generate API key + secret → add ALPACA_KEY and ALPACA_SECRET to .env. Test with a simple REST call: GET /v2/account. Paper trading is 100% free with simulated $100k. No live money needed to start development.

todos.title: Trading bot — build data pipeline: news + whale + politician signals
todos.notes: Three signal feeds to wire up: (1) News: poll NewsAPI or Benzinga every 5 min for ticker-tagged headlines; score sentiment. (2) Whale alerts: Unusual Whales API for large options prints and dark pool trades above a threshold; consider Twitter/X @unusual_whales as free alternative. (3) Politician trades: poll Quiver Quantitative /congresstrading endpoint daily; flag trades made by politicians in sectors relevant to pending legislation. Store signals in a local SQLite or JSON log.

todos.title: Define Atlas DevOps routine — GitHub Actions vs alternatives
todos.notes: Research and decide: (1) Is GitHub Actions the right CI/CD layer for a local-first personal system like Atlas? Alternatives: act (local GH Actions), Taskfile, custom cron + shell scripts, Gitea CI. (2) What checks should run on every commit: lint, npm audit, docker build test, route smoke tests? (3) Output a recommended workflow file or Taskfile. Existing todo_20260502_002 covers the narrow npm audit case — this is the broader design question.

todos.title: Find proper form guide visuals for 5 placeholder exercises
todos.notes: Five exercises currently show text-only cue card placeholders (replaced mismatched custom SVGs): Deadlift, Dead Hang, Wrist Curls, Reverse Wrist Curls, Plank. Options: (1) Find Everkinetic PNGs on Wikimedia Commons that match the line-drawing style — apply filter:invert(1) brightness(0.85) like the other exercises. (2) If no Everkinetic image exists, redesign the SVGs to match the inverted line-drawing aesthetic. Download any PNGs to public/img/exercises/ and add entries to attribution.json.

todos.title: Fitness app market study — features, UX patterns, user sentiment
todos.notes: Research what established gym/fitness apps offer and what users want. Cover: (1) Feature comparison — what do MyFitnessPal, Hevy, Strong, FitBod, Garmin Connect, Apple Fitness+ include? (2) User sentiment — what do users love vs. complain about in reviews (App Store, Reddit r/fitness, r/homegym)? (3) Most-requested features — what gaps exist? (4) UX patterns — how do top apps handle exercise selection, progress tracking, workout logging, body diagrams? (5) Output: structured report in output/ with recommendations for Atlas training module. DONE: output/fitness_app_market_study.md written 2026-05-04.

todos.title: Research open-source anatomical body models for anatomy mode
todos.notes: Find a medical-grade 3D or SVG body model where EVERY muscle can be individually highlighted. Open-source and CC-licensed only — no commercial software. Targets: (1) GitHub projects for anatomy visualization — search 'human anatomy SVG', 'muscle map browser', '3D anatomy open source'; (2) BodyParts3D (CC BY from RIKEN); (3) CC-licensed SVG anatomy atlases from medical education resources; (4) Any Three.js/WebGL anatomy viewer projects. Must support individual muscle selection/highlight programmatically, ideally with Latin muscle names. Report: list candidates, license, integration complexity, browser-compatibility. Output to output/anatomical_model_research.md.

todos.title: Anatomy mode toggle for training module
todos.notes: Add a toggle (button top-right of detail panel or page-level) that switches between 'Standard' and 'Anatomy' mode. Anatomy mode: (1) Replace current BodyMuscles body diagram with a full anatomical model where every muscle lights up (depends on todo_20260504_013 research). (2) Style switch — biology textbook / medical learning aesthetic: off-white/cream background, serif font for labels, anatomical illustration style, muted clinical color palette. (3) All muscle names displayed in Latin (e.g. 'chest' → 'Pectoralis Major', 'lats' → 'Latissimus Dorsi'). (4) Color shading by muscle role: primary muscles brighter, secondary/stabilisers dimmer. Prerequisite: todo_20260504_013 must complete first.

todos.title: Exercise library: clickable muscle model with reverse lookup
todos.notes: On the /exercises page (todo_20260504_006), add an interactive body model in the left panel. UX: (1) Click a muscle on the model → list of exercises that train it appears in a panel. (2) Click an exercise from that list → all muscles it trains light up on the model. (3) Dual-color scheme: the originally clicked muscle = one color (e.g. teal), the other muscles lit up by the selected exercise = a second color (e.g. orange/amber) — so user can see 'this is the muscle I chose' vs 'these are the bonus muscles'. (4) In anatomy mode (todo_20260504_014) show Latin names as hover tooltips on the model. This is the core UX of the exercise library page — build it alongside todo_20260504_006.

todos.title: Fix Plank form guide image — currently a text placeholder
todos.notes: The Plank_exercise.svg from Wikimedia is a solid-colored SVG (blue/teal silhouette) that doesn't match the Everkinetic line-drawing style. The custom SVG schematic also clashed with the overall design. Now replaced by a text cue card placeholder (2026-05-04). Folded into todo_20260504_011 — find a proper visual solution there.
